global_rules: Start from a freshly fetched origin/dev and work on task/<task-id>.
One auto worker owns one complete task at a time; dependent follow-up work is not claimable until the current task is terminal.
The approved schema, state, OpenAPI, authorization, event, privacy, reliability, and rollout artifacts are binding and must not be reinterpreted by handlers or UI code.
Contract-first tasks ODP-INTAKE-SCHEMA-001 through ODP-INTAKE-EVENTS-001 must pass before dependent product behavior is claimable.
Every task opens a PR to dev and requires a different reviewer; the owner may not self-finalize.
Documentation, fixtures, mocks, static token checks, or deterministic simulations do not prove runtime completion.
Production paths remain fail-closed. Missing credentials or external infrastructure produce an honest blocker after all locally implementable work is complete.
Evidence under docs/evidence/completion/<task-id>/ records exact commands, results, source SHA, limitations, and reviewer findings.

tasks.title: Implement assisted intake relational schema and tenant isolation
acceptance: Convert the approved four-artifact PostgreSQL DDL stack into an ordered production migration without dropping approved constraints.
Implement every approved type, enum, nullability rule, foreign key, tenant-qualified unique constraint, check, version column, index, retention field, legal-hold field, and authoritative timestamp.
Enable and FORCE RLS on every tenant-bearing table with fail-closed tenant isolation policies.
Prove clean install, upgrade, downgrade boundary, catalog constraints, tenant isolation, and rollback behavior on PostgreSQL 16.

tasks.title: Implement binding intake, listing, decision, assignment, and SLA state engines
acceptance: Implement every legal transition and reject every absent transition in the approved per-transition tables.
Enforce actor, permission, precondition, idempotency, concurrency token, persisted evidence, retry result, terminal, and reopen semantics.
Implement assignment claim, transfer, SLA due time, overdue, escalation, completion, and concurrent-owner behavior.
Provide exhaustive table-driven tests for intake, listing, decision, assignment/SLA, and promotion orchestration states.

tasks.title: Implement reversible listing identity graph
acceptance: Persist immutable identity edges with effective and superseded selection plus canonical property redirects.
Implement merge, split, unmerge, reversal, cycle prevention, concurrency conflict, and partial-operation rollback.
Keep source records immutable and resolve every listing and candidate reference without losing lineage.
Expose deterministic lineage and effective-identity queries with tenant isolation.
verification: python3 -m ruff check modules/integration modules/listing tests/unit/listing/test_identity_graph.py tests/integration/test_assisted_listing_identity.py

tasks.title: Implement assisted intake v1 API and generated client
acceptance: Build the effective OpenAPI by applying all five overlays in manifest order and generate client/types from that artifact.
Implement query, URL/batch intake, correction, decision, merge/split/unmerge, assignment, retry, and promotion endpoints without inventing payloads.
Enforce cursor, masking, idempotency, If-Match, replay, partial-success, conflict, authorization, and declared error schemas.
Add request/response and generated-client drift tests for every operation.

tasks.title: Implement deny-by-default intake authorization and segregation
acceptance: Enforce the approved role/action/resource/scope/state/field/risk matrix in backend policy with deny-by-default behavior.
Implement tenant, brand, region, area, HeatZone, workflow state, field classification, and ownership checks.
Enforce segregation of duties, self-review prohibition, emergency administration restrictions, masking, export, purge, and legal-hold permissions.
Return the exact approved denial or masking reason code and record attributable audit evidence.
verification: python3 -m ruff check shared/auth modules/opsboard/auth modules/listing/application/intake_authorization.py tests/security

tasks.title: Implement typed intake events, outbox, deduplication, replay, and DLQ
acceptance: Implement the approved versioned envelope and every registered payload schema.
Bind producer, transaction owner, aggregate and partition key, ordering scope, consumers, sensitive-field policy, and compatibility behavior.
Implement transactional outbox, consumer deduplication lifetime, bounded retry, DLQ, retention, replay authority, and deprecation behavior.
External webhooks remain explicitly unsupported in v1 and no webhook delivery path is exposed.
verification: python3 -m ruff check shared/domain/events.py shared/infrastructure/persistence/outbox.py apps/worker/consumers tests

tasks.title: Implement source snapshot provenance, checksum, residency, and reconciliation
acceptance: Store raw or redacted immutable snapshots with generation, checksum, observed/captured times, parser input reference, and retention class.
Enforce source policy before retrieval and fail closed for unknown, blocked, authentication-required, or assisted-entry-only sources.
Enforce TW_ONLY residency, access/export restrictions, legal hold, and correction lineage.
Implement SQL/GCS consistency reconciliation, missing-object quarantine, and idempotent recovery.
verification: python3 -m ruff check modules/external_data shared/infrastructure/object_store tests

tasks.title: Implement durable intake jobs with fencing, timeout, backpressure, and cancellation
acceptance: Implement durable job identity, lease and fencing tokens, heartbeat, stage timeout, retry/backoff, acknowledgement, and lost-response recovery.
Implement queue concurrency and rate limits, backpressure thresholds, poison isolation, DLQ retention, authorized replay, and cancellation checkpoints.
Prevent stale workers and duplicate delivery from producing duplicate outcomes.
Emit the approved metrics, alerts, audit events, and operator evidence for every failure path.
verification: python3 -m ruff check shared/jobs shared/infrastructure/persistence/job_queue.py apps/worker/assisted_listing_intake tests

tasks.title: Implement intake purge, legal hold, evidence export, and WORM integrity
acceptance: Implement purge execution, legal-hold placement/release, residency enforcement, subject/export scope, and deletion-conflict fail-closed behavior.
Generate watermarked exports, manifests, checksums, signer/key versions, verification results, and download evidence.
Protect immutable decision and source evidence with the approved WORM boundary and audit-failure behavior.
Prove unauthorized purge/export denial, hold protection, manifest verification, and retention execution.

tasks.title: Implement reviewed listing-to-candidate promotion saga
acceptance: Remove automatic candidate creation and require explicit request plus independent review before execution.
Implement idempotent Intake to Listing to Candidate Site to SiteScore orchestration with duplicate-candidate prevention.
Define authoritative success, partial failure, compensation, retry, scoring-failure, reversal, and audit outcomes.
High-impact actions remain non-optimistic and enforce If-Match plus segregation of duties.

tasks.title: Implement assisted listing intake workflow UI
acceptance: Implement URL submission, durable processing detail, parsed-field correction, duplicate/revision comparison, assisted entry, and Listing Inbox integration.
Render only approved stages, outcomes, permissions, conflicts, masking, timestamps, freshness, error codes, and next actions.
Implement all required empty, loading, partial, failure, retry, blocked, read-only, stale, and quarantined states without silent fixture fallback.
Prove keyboard, focus, screen-reader change summary, desktop comparison, tablet confirmation, and mobile tracking behavior.

tasks.title: Implement staging backfill, reconciliation, and rollback
acceptance: Implement versioned mapping, tenant/source/month backfill, dry run, resume, reconciliation, and irreconcilable-record ownership.
Preserve source, parser, correction, decision, identity, listing revision, candidate, and audit lineage without fabrication.
Produce count/checksum proof and shadow comparison before cutover.
Implement rollback and forward-recovery runbooks with executable staging evidence.

tasks.title: Validate assisted intake capacity, SLO, recovery, and error budget
acceptance: Measure approved intake volume, batch size, concurrency, queue age, latency, parser completion, review SLA, and availability targets.
Exercise provider latency, duplicate delivery, worker loss, SQL failover, GCS inconsistency, queue backlog, retry budget, and DLQ recovery.
Drive SLO and error-budget evidence from measured runtime data rather than static checks.
Record measured RPO/RTO and identify every missed target without weakening acceptance.
verification: python3 -m ruff check tests/performance/assisted_listing_intake tests/reliability/assisted_listing_intake scripts/load/assisted_listing_intake scripts/chaos/assisted_listing_intake

tasks.title: Execute tenant and source canary, UAT, restore, rollback, and governed cutover
acceptance: Run shadow processing, tenant/source canary, UAT, migration reconciliation, kill switch, rollback trigger, and release-authority gates.
Verify no legacy, SQLite, memory, fixture, or silent fallback path is presented as production-ready.
Execute restore and rollback drills in the required order and reconcile SQL, GCS, queues, events, and audit evidence.
Keep every production flag disabled until all section 12 owner approvals and exact runtime evidence are recorded.
